items.title: An expert from the Guardia di Finanza at the Italian Embassy in Riyadh
items.description: (ANSA) - IL CAIRO, 19 LUG - The Italian Embassy in Riyadh has strengthened its structure with the establishment of a Guardia di Finanza Office, headed by Colonel Alessandro Coscarelli in his capacity as an expert from the Corps. The opening of the new office represents an important step in consolidating the partnership between Italy and Saudi Arabia, with the goal of strengthening cooperation in the field of economic and financial security. It joins the other 29 Financial Police Expert offices currently part of the Corps’ overseas network at bilateral and multilateral diplomatic missions. The
items.titleIt: An expert from the Guardia di Finanza at the Italian Embassy in Riyadh

items.title: University Mission to Dakar for Psychology Education in Senegal
items.description: (ANSA) - IL CAIRO, 16 LUG - The Italian Ambassador to Dakar, Michele Tommasi, met with a delegation from the “G. d’Annunzio” University of Chieti-Pescara, which was on an institutional mission to Senegal as part of the preparation of an Erasmus+ Capacity Building in Higher Education project proposal. The mission, which involves Italian, French, and Senegalese partners, aims to strengthen university and scientific cooperation in the field of psychology in Senegal, with a particular focus on the development of education and research. The project is coordinated by Université Paris Cité (France) a
items.titleIt: University Mission to Dakar for Psychology Education in Senegal

items.title: Egypt receive a warm welcome home
items.description: Watch as the Egyptian football team return to a hero's welcome from their fans.
items.titleIt: Egypt receive a warm welcome home

items.title: New Chapter, Same Stalemate: Somalia’s War with Al-Shabaab
items.description: The Somali government’s battle with Al-Shabaab militants grinds on, with neither able to gain a decisive advantage. To improve its lot, Mogadishu needs to bolster its military and alleviate civilian suffering in areas under insurgent control, while looking for openings for dialogue with the group.
items.titleIt: New Chapter, Same Stalemate: Somalia’s War with Al-Shabaab

items.title: “Strategic Balance” under Stress: Egypt and the U.S.-Israeli War with Iran
items.description: Egypt has been walking a tightrope since the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran began, joining mediation efforts while signalling solidarity with Gulf Arab partners under Iranian attack. But with some Gulf capitals voicing expectations of stronger support, Cairo may find itself under increasing pressure.
items.titleIt: “Strategic Balance” under Stress: Egypt and the U.S.-Israeli War with Iran

items.title: Kenya: Ruto Warned Against Underestimating Gachagua
items.description: [Capital FM] Nairobi -- Former Law Society of Kenya (LSK) President Nelson Havi has warned President William Ruto and leaders of the Broad-Based Government against dismissing Rigathi Gachagua's growing political influence, arguing that the Ol Kalou by-election has demonstrated the former Deputy President's strength in the Mt Kenya region.
items.titleIt: Kenya: Ruto Warned Against Underestimating Gachagua

items.title: Ethiopians march against alleged forced mobilisation in Tigray
items.description: Thousands rally in Ethiopia's capital Addis Ababa to protest the alleged forced recruitment of civilians in the Tigray region by Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF) forces, the breakaway region's main political party, amid months of escalating tensions with Ethiopia's federal government
items.titleIt: Ethiopians march against alleged forced mobilisation in Tigray

items.title: GDP up 0.4% Q2, inflation returns to 2.5% in July says Confcommercio
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUL 17 - Italy's GDP was up 0.4% in the second quarter of this tear while inflation fell back to 2.5% in July, retail group Confcommercio said Friday. The geopolitical scenario, after a brief period of respite, has seen renewed tensions in the Middle East, making forecasts for the Italian economy "slightly more uncertain and complex," "still experiencing undeniable signs of liveliness overall," Confcommercio stated in its latest report, estimating a second quarter of further growth of 0.4%, with a 1.2% increase compared to the same period in 2025. The growth achieved to date all
items.titleIt: GDP up 0.4% Q2, inflation returns to 2.5% in July says Confcommercio

items.title: Sanctions on Moscow won't stop war, hope there aren't another 20 packages - Salvini
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUL 16 - Right-wing League party leader, Deputy Premier and Transport Minister Matteo Salvini said Thursday that European Union sanctions on Moscow would not stop the UKraine war and he hoped there would not be another 20 packages of them. "It doesn't seem to me that the sanctions have brought an end to the conflict... and I hope there aren't another 20 sanctions packages before we reach the end of this war. At the negotiating table, because this conflict won't end with a clear winner," Salvini said this during a video conference at a meeting of Gim Unimpresa, the association of
items.titleIt: Sanctions on Moscow won't stop war, hope there aren't another 20 packages - Salvini

items.title: Villa warned over sportswashing after Visit Rwanda deal
items.description: Aston Villa are warned their new £20m-a-year sponsorship deal with Visit Rwanda will be used by the nation to sportswash its human rights record.
items.titleIt: Villa warned over sportswashing after Visit Rwanda deal
